title: Docker (DevOps view)
objectives: Use Docker to build reproducible CI environments
Bake images with the right base + cache
Ship images to a registry with security scanning

sections.markdown: For DevOps, Docker is your **portable build/run environment**. You ship the app + its runtime as one image; CI, staging and prod use the same artifact — solving 'works on my machine' for both dev and ops.

sections.code:
# CI image with cached deps
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk AS build
WORKDIR /src
COPY pom.xml .           # dep manifest first
RUN mvn -B -o dependency:go-offline
COPY . .
RUN mvn -B package -DskipTests

FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine
COPY --from=build /src/target/*.jar /app/app.jar
USER 1000
ENTRYPOINT ["java","-jar","/app/app.jar"]

rows: Tiny final image; leave build tools behind

sections.markdown: For CI/CD, images are your **hermetic build environment**. Push each build's image to a registry with a mutable + immutable tag (`v1.2.3` + `sha-abc123`), scan for CVEs, and promote the SAME image from staging to prod — never rebuild for prod.
Rebuilding for each environment. Every rebuild risks non-determinism (deps updated, base image drift). Build once → promote the same digest across environments.

Use buildx for multi-arch (amd64 + arm64) and BuildKit cache mounts for even faster CI. Store images in a private registry (ECR/GCR/GHCR); enable image signing (cosign) if you care about supply chain.

flashcards.back: Build one image, tag it, promote the SAME digest through staging → prod. Never rebuild for prod.
Immutable tag (sha-abc) + mutable version tag (v1.2.3); always promote by digest.
Order layers by change frequency, use BuildKit cache mounts, and multi-stage builds.

quiz.q: Best base for a small Java runtime?

challenges.text: Write a Dockerfile for your Spring Boot app; run it locally.
Push to GHCR from GitHub Actions with a versioned + immutable tag.
Add Trivy scanning and fail build on HIGH/CRITICAL CVEs.
Multi-arch build with buildx + cosign signing.

interviewQuestions: How do you keep images small and secure?
How do you cache in CI?
